scope: Authored viewport appearance only. The source has no assigned material, filament shader, slicer data, or G-code toolpath for this object.

capture.lightScaleInterpretation: Blender Area energy and Three.js RectAreaLight intensity are renderer-specific units. A light sweep through 30, 45, 90, 180, 360, and 720 showed a monotonic convergence toward Blender's clipped-white neutral surface; 720 is the closest recorded capture before the remaining edge/backface residual dominates.

interpretation: The source and GN-VM outputs have matching topology and near-identical aligned silhouettes. Because Blender's Medium High Contrast reference clips 99.999% of this unassigned neutral surface white, luminance correlation is not informative; occupancy, silhouette, and absolute RGB error are the useful measures. WebGL reproduces the neutral fallback with 0.0138 RGB MAE, with the remaining dark rim attributable to Eevee/Three.js lighting and backface differences.
